data.title: Laurentian Bank of Canada reports Q3 2025 net income of $37.5M, EPS $0.73, ROE 5.0%. Adjusted net income $39.6M, EPS $0.78.
data.description: The Laurentian Bank of Canada, in Montreal, reported its financial results for the third quarter of 2025. The bank's net income for this quarter was $37.5 million with diluted earnings per share of $0.73, compared to $34.1 million and $0.67 for the same period in 2024. The return on common shareholders' equity was 5.0% in Q3 2025, up from 4.7% in Q3 2024. Adjusted net income was $39.6 million with adjusted diluted earnings per share of $0.78. For the nine months ended July 31, 2025, reported net income was $108.4 million compared to a net loss of $46.2 million in 2024. Diluted earnings per share for the nine-month period were $2.17. Adjusted net income for the same period was $113.0 million with adjusted diluted earnings per share of $2.28. Adjusted return on common shareholders' equity for the nine months was 5.3%. The bank's President and CEO, Éric Provost, attributed the bank's strong performance to its market position, risk management, liquidity, and capital strength, as well as the team's commitment and strategic execution.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson discontinues rheumatoid arthritis drug combo program after Phase 2a study with nipocalimab and anti-TNFα therapy. RA causes pain and swelling in joints. J&J spent $6.5B to acquire Momenta Pharmaceuticals for nipocalimab in 2020.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson recently disclosed preliminary results from the Phase 2a DAISY study, which explored the potential of combining nipocalimab with an anti-TNFα therapy in rheumatoid arthritis patients with refractory disease. Rheumatoid arthritis is a chronic autoimmune condition that triggers joint inflammation and pain, primarily affecting the hands and wrists on both sides of the body. In 2020, Johnson & Johnson invested a substantial $6.5 billion in acquiring Momenta Pharmaceuticals, allowing them to access nipocalimab, the company's flagship product. Notably, Johnson & Johnson also pledged $2 billion towards expanding US manufacturing operations amidst concerns about potential drug tariffs. data.title: Natural Grocers in Centennial, CO to celebrate remodel with savings from Sept. 12-Oct. 31. Giveaways, samples, sweepstakes included.
data.description: Beginning on September 12 and running through October 31, customers can benefit from significant discounts at the recently renovated store in LAKEWOOD, Colo. Natural Grocers®, the primary family-owned organic and natural grocery store in the U.S., is hosting this event. Along with exclusive savings, customers can also participate in giveaways, sweepstakes, and receive complimentary samples. This initiative aims to provide an enhanced shopping experience and foster community engagement at the store.

data.title: XRP price drops 4% to $2.88, testing critical support level; recent partnerships drive adoption.
data.description: The digital currency XRP is currently priced at $2.88, experiencing a 4.12% decrease in trading value. This decline has led to testing the lower Bollinger Band support level. Despite this, the cryptocurrency has been gaining adoption through recent collaborations with SBI Holdings and TRM Labs.
